She was also instrumental to: 1. Forming the Association for Social Research (ASR) during India's post-colonial era focusing primarily towards understanding societal changes. 2. texts.full_text: used are Agathosma betulina (Thunberg) Bartling et Wendland; A. crenulata (Linné) Hooker; and A. serratifolia Thunberg. The first is the official buchu leaf of the British Pharmacopoeia, while that of the United States Pharmacopœia consists chiefly of a mixture of the two last-named species. The root of Agathosma betulina has been used in medicine under the name of Buchu Root (see p. 1042). The leaves are collected from wild plants at any time during their flowering period, which extends throughout the year, and dried before they are marketed. They have a strong aromatic odor resembling that of camphor, with an intensely bitter taste. The leaf is small, linear-lanceolate or lanceolate in form, measuring about 1 inch long by 3/8 inch wide; it has a short petiole and entire margin, the surface being smooth and shiny green above while beneath it is and of a lighter shade. The midrib on both surfaces is conspicuously raised. The leaf is cut into pieces before being packed for market in bales or bags weighing 100 to 250 pounds each. It does not deteriorate when kept dry, but becomes moldy if exposed to damp air. When fresh it contains about 8 per cent of volatile oil and a large amount of tannin and resinous matter; the latter is removed by decoction with water, after which the leaf yields from 4 to 6 per cent of its weight in volatile oil upon distillation. The leaves contain an aromatic principle called buchu camphor (C10H18O), having a strong odor resembling that of camphor and yielding on oxidation a crystalline acid melting at 235° F., which is probably the same as menthol, C10H20O, found in peppermint oil. The leaves also contain tannic acid, resinous matter, fatty substances, and an acrid principle called buchuin (C24H38N2), isomeric with conine of hemlock, but having a bitter taste instead of the peculiar narcotic odor characteristic of that alkaloid. The volatile oil is pale yellow in color, has a strong aromatic odor resembling camphor, and yields on distillation a crystalline substance melting at 19

If not, you probably have more flexibility than you’ve been taking advantage of. Make a list of adventures Once you’ve convinced yourself of your freedom, it’s time to figure out what you’d like to do with it. what little adventures might work best between 9 and 5? What life maintenance activities would be easier to do during business hours? You could exercise outside while it’s light or visit the gym when it’s less crowded (important with Covid-19). Some houses of worship are open for during-the-day prayers. A local historic site or museum might welcome visitors between, say, 9 a.m. and 2 p.m. You might also think about your during-the-day energy levels. Would you like to spend an afternoon hour reading outside or maybe taking a nap? Maybe you’d like to spend time with your kids right when the school day ends. When you know how you want to use your time, you’ll be more motivated to make it happen. Make a schedule Look at your calendar and choose an upcoming day to try working a different schedule. No meetings on Tuesday? Try reserving a timed ticket for that art museum at 11:30 a.m. Plan to sign off for two hours or so, then figure out when you can make up the time to still hit roughly eight hours for the day. Maybe you start at 7:30 a.m. and work until 11 a.m. and then again from 1 to 5:30 p.m. to hit eight hours. Alternately, you could create three shifts: the morning, 1–4:30 p.m., and then 8–9 p.m. after your kids go to bed. You might not need all this time, and you might decide to make up the time on some other day instead of hitting eight hours on any individual day, but creating a schedule like this helps with mental accounting (and allows you to proactively communicate to your co-workers when you’ll be unreachable, if necessary). texts.full_text: here is none.Both superintendent finalists for the Wachusett Regional School District withdrew their names from consideration for the post Tuesday.And at a special meeting Tuesday night of the Wachusett Regional School Committee, a motion to name district Director of Operations Darryll McCall as a finalist was withdrawn, leaving the committee with no candidates — and no superintendent as of Aug. 31. School committee Chairman Duncan Leith recommended that the search for a new superintendent be left to the next school committee, which will have at least six new members following the annual town elections in the district later this month. In his recommendation, Mr. Leith noted the significant changes coming with the new committee and suggested it would be beneficial for them to lead this critical process. “We’ve had a challenging time finding candidates who can meet our high standards,” said Mr. Leith. “I think it’s best that we allow the new school committee members, who will have the fresh perspectives of their constituents, to take on this important task.” The committee voted unanimously to accept Mr. Leith's recommendation, agreeing to pause the search and reconvene after the elections to reassess their needs and the candidates. Meanwhile, Assistant Superintendent John O’Donnell has been serving as interim superintendent since July 2012. The school committee expressed its gratitude for his continued service and unanimously agreed that he would remain in this role until a permanent replacement is found. "We are fortunate to have Mr. O'Donnell’s leadership during this transitional period," added Mr. Leith. "His familiarity with the district will help ensure stability while we navigate these changes." It was also one of Newt Gingrich's final acts as speaker. As a service to our readers, every day we are delivering a classic moment from the political life of Newt Gingrich—until he either clinches the nomination or bows out. Newt Gingrich called his 1998 swing through Alaska's North Slope "an eye-opening experience" that helped him better understand the challenges that environmental regulations pose to residents of the Last Frontier. "Don Young has been telling me for years—come to Alaska and see for yourself," he said, of the state's Republican congressman. "Seeing is believing!" CrWhat Newt Gingrich experienced on his trip to Alaska's North Slope in 1998 was indeed eye-opening, but perhaps not in the way he expected. The "blanket-tossing" he referred to was a traditional Inuit practice, where a person is gently thrown into the air on a blanket, a ritual often reserved for special occasions or to honor a guest. During his visit, Gingrich was part of a delegation that included several Congress members and was hosted by the local Inuit community. The blanket-tossing was a cultural experience meant to welcome and honor the visitors. However, the event took a controversial turn when Gingrich used the opportunity to deliver a political speech, advocating for increased oil drilling in the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge (ANWR). This incident highlighted the tension between cultural understanding and political agendas. While Gingrich might have genuinely enjoyed the cultural experience, his immediate focus on drilling in a protected area, a controversial topic even today, overshadowed the goodwill and cultural exchange intended by the blanket-tossing ritual. texts.full_text: d can be tailored to fit the specific needs of each patient, using various methods such as intensity-modulated radiation therapy (IMRT), image-guided radiation therapy (IGRT), proton therapy, and stereotactic body radiotherapy (SBRT). These advanced techniques allow for higher doses of radiation to be delivered directly to tumors while minimizing damage to nearby healthy organs and tissues. The planning process for radiation therapy is meticulous. It often involves detailed imaging scans, such as CT, MRI, or PET scans, to accurately map the location of the tumor and surrounding structures. This information is used to create a personalized treatment plan that optimizes the delivery of radiation while protecting vital areas. Treatment sessions are typically given over several weeks, with each session lasting only a few minutes. Despite its effectiveness, radiation therapy can cause side effects, which vary depending on the part of the body being treated and the type of radiation used. Common side effects include fatigue, skin changes (like redness or irritation), and in some cases, more specific issues like difficulty swallowing if the head and neck area is targeted. However, many of these side effects can be managed with supportive care, and they often diminish after treatment ends. texts.full_text: expanded using various methods such as balloon expansion or self-expansion mechanisms. Balloon catheters are commonly used for deploying these types of expandable grafts, particularly those that require precise and controlled radial force application. The typical design involves a deflated balloon attached to the distal end of the delivery system which is navigated through blood vessels until it reaches its target location within or near an occluded segment. Upon reaching this point, inflation fluid (often contrast media) under pressure expands both the stent as well as dilates any existing plaque buildup against vessel walls. However, challenges arise during deployment due to uneven expansion forces that can lead to malapposition—where parts of a deployed device do not contact or conform adequately with surrounding tissue—or excessive force causing damage like dissection (tearing) within arterial linings. To address these issues and improve outcomes for patients undergoing stenting procedures: 1. Balloon Design Enhancements: Innovations in balloon materials, coatings, shapes, tapers have been developed to ensure more consistent inflation profiles across the entire surface area of a given device. 2. Controlled Deployment Mechanisms: Advanced catheter designs incorporate features like dual-lumen constructions allowing independent control over different sections during expansion; this helps achieve better conformity and reduces risks associated with abrupt changes in pressure application points along its lengthwise axis when inflated fully at once versus incrementally from one end to another gradually. 3. Optimized Stent-Catheter Integration: Ensuring secure attachment between the stents themselves alongside their delivery platforms through improved manufacturing processes or novel locking mechanisms ensures they remain stable throughout transit and deployment phases without slippage which could otherwise result in misalignment post-placement inside target sites. texts.full_text: tissues. A high pressure liquid chromatographic (HPLC) procedure is presented for the determination of nitrofurazone and furazolidone in chicken and pork tissues in the 2-40 ppb range. Muscle, liver, and kidney are homogenized with cold methanol and water (50 + 50). Following methanol evaporation, the nitrofurans are partitioned into ethyl acetate and cleaned up on an alumina column. After elution with 20% methanol in ethyl acetate and evaporation to dryness, residues are determined by HPLC, using a reverse phase analytical column.The development of the high-pressure liquid chromatographic (HPLC) method for quantifying nitrofurazone and furazolidone in poultry meat is an essential step toward ensuring food safety, regulatory compliance with maximum residue limits set by health authorities. This analytical technique provides a sensitive means to detect low concentrations that may be present due to veterinary drug use or contamination. Procedure Overview: 1. Sample Preparation - Chicken and pork tissues (muscle, liver, kidney) are homogenized using cold methanol for extraction. 2. Extraction Process - The mixture is then diluted with water in a ratio of 50 + 40 parts by volume to enhance the recovery process. 3. Partitioning Step: - Post-extraction and solvent evaporation, nitrofurazone residues are partitioned into ethyl acetate. 4. Cleanup Procedure: - The organic phase is passed through an alumina column to remove any interfering substances. 5. HPLC Analysis: The purified compounds undergo reversed-phase high-performance liquid chromatography (RP-HPLC) for separation and quantification using a suitable analytical gradient system with ultraviolet detection

texts.full_text: r Union defendants-appellees. Before McWILLIAMS, BARRETT and McKAY, Circuit Judges. McKAY, Circuit Judge. 1 Seven employees or former employees of the Southern Pacific Transportation Company (Railroad) brought this suit against the Railroad and three labor unions, alleging that the unions and the Railroad conspired to deny the employees their seniority and other employment rights. The employees claimed that the Railroad and the unions violated the Railway Labor Act (RLA) and the Labor Management Relations Act (LMRA) and conspired to deny the employees their rights under the fifth and fourteenth amendments. The employees sought to recover damages and to obtain injunctive and declaratory relief. The unions and the Railroad moved to dismiss the employees' complaint. The district court granted the motions and dismissed the complaint. The employees appeal. We affirm. I. Background A. The employees' complaint alleges the following facts. The Railroad employs the employees as carmen and carmen helpers. The Brotherhood of Railway Carmen of the United States and Canada (National Union) and the System Federation No. 114, Railway Employees Department, AFL-CIO (System Federation) are the certified representatives of the Railroad's carmen and carmen helpers. The employees are members of the National Union and the System Federation. The National Union and the System Federation, along with the Railroad, are parties to a collective bargaining agreement. The agreement establishes the employees' seniority and other employment rights. Since then, numerous conferences and workshops have been organized toaddress this critical topic, involving not only government agencies but also the private sector and civil society organizations. The impact of climate change on agriculture is multifaceted and far-reaching, affecting crop yields, water availability, soil health, and biodiversity. Moreover, the increasing frequency and severity of extreme weather events threaten global food systems, putting pressure on already fragile food security networks. To mitigate these effects, it is essential to adopt sustainableagricultural practices, such as regenerative agriculture, agroforestry, and conservation agriculture, which notonlyreduce greenhouse gas emissions but also improve soil health, increase biodiversity, and enhance ecosystem services. texts.full_text: to a pool party that got out of control, thanks to media. A Texas housing manager is facing a huge fine for his bash after a hash tag went viral online, drawing 2,000 people to his pool party. ABC's nick watt had the story. Reporter: Here's what happens when a media marketing campaign is just too successful. It was insane. It was packed. All you could see were heads everywhere. People in the pool. Reporter: Last Saturday, 2,000 reportedly boozy revelers, showed up in a pool designedfor just 75 people, at a Texas housing complex, after a party invitation went viral on social media, complete with a hashtag that spread like wildfire. The party was supposed to be a small, intimate gathering, but it quickly spiraled out of control, with attendees spilling out of the pool area and into the surrounding streets. Now, the housing manager who hosted the party is facing a hefty fine, and possibly even eviction, for violating the complex's rules and causing a disturbance. The partygoers, many of whom were underage, left behind a trail of destruction, including glass, overturned furniture, and a heavily damaged pool area. Authorities are still trying to determine how the party got so out of hand, and how to prevent similar incidents in the future. The housing manager, who has not been named, has apologized for the incident and promised to cooperate with the

texts.full_text: near work task, fellow eyes also displayed a symmetrical change in superior corneal topography (hyperopic defocus) which correlated with the position of the upper eyelid during downward gaze. Greater changes in the spherical corneal power vector (M) following reading were associated with a narrower palpebral aperture during downward gaze (p = 0.07 for more myopic and p = 0.03 for less myopic eyes). A significantly greater change in J0 (an increase indicating the amount of corneal surface astigmatism) was observed in the more myopic eye (p = 0.05) while no significant change was noted in the less myopic eye. The study suggests that corneal changes following near work are not only limited to the more myopic eye but also occur in the less myopic eye, although the magnitude of the change may be different between the eyes. These findings may have implications for understanding the development of anisometropic amblyopia and for designing therapeutic interventions aimed at reducing the progression of myopia in children with anisometropia. The transition from Recruiters to Career Consultants signifies a shift towards personalized career development, offering clients more comprehensive support throughout their assignment process – including pre-assignment planning guidance on how assignments can be tailored for personal growth. Their expertise in healthcare staffing also extends beyond matching opportunities; they serve as trusted advisors who help professionals navigate the complexities of travel nursing and allied health roles. Anderson continued: "Our Career Consultants go above just finding an assignment – we see it through from start to finish, ensuring our travelers are well-supported every step along their journey. It's about partnering with us for career growth rather than simply being a temporary work arrangement." The rebrand aims not only at reflecting the full range of services offered but also emphasizes Medical Solutions' commitment towards building long-term relationships and fostering professional development among its talent base. In this new role, Career Consultants are expected to provide ongoing support that includes: 1. Career Path Development: Offering tailored career advice based on individual aspirations. 2. Market Insights & Trends Analysis: Keeping travelers informed about the latest industry trends and opportunities for professional growth within healthcare staffing roles across various specialties, locations or work environments (e.g., hospital-based vs clinic settings). 3. Cultural and Physical Adjustment Support: Facilitating a smooth transition to new assignments by providing insights into local cultures as well as assisting with logistical concerns such as relocation support. 4. Continued Professional Growth Opportunities: Identifying opportunities for continuous learning, including advanced certifications or specialized training that aligns the traveler's skills and interests. By embracing these responsibilities more openly through their new title of Career Consultants – a nod to both personal career development and professional networking within healthcare staffing environments worldwide - Medical Solutions continues its mission in enhancing patient care outcomes by ensuring top-tier talent is always available where it’s needed most. This rebrand solidifies the company's dedication towards fostering meaningful relationships with their travelers, providing them not just jobs but possibilities for long-term career advancement and fulfillment. Not until 1967 were miscegenation laws taken off the books. Involuntary Sterilization In 1907 Indiana enacted a law allowing the forced sterilization of individuals on genetic grounds. In 1914 Harry Laughlin published the "Model Eugenical Sterilization Law." By that time twelve states had already enacted sterilization laws. Laughlin proposed the authorization of involuntary sterilization of those who were "Socially inadequate" or who were "maintained wholly or in part by public expense" (Welfare, Prison, Mental Hospitals, orphanages, ect...) as well as the "feebleminded, insane, criminalistic, epileptic, inebriate, diseased, blind, deaf; deformed; and dependent {...} orphans, ne'er-do-wells, tramps, the homeless and paupers." In 1924 Virginia passed a sterilization law based on Laughlin's model, and approximately 3,000 people had been involuntarily sterilized up to that point. Virginia supported the idea that all social problems were the result of genetically inferior people and that is was necessary to sterilize "Defective persons" whose reproduction posed a "Menace to society." Legal documents show that doctors were often called into court to testify about the character of persons whom they had never met by virtue of medical bases. Any concept of evidence in a sterilization trial was a mockery of the legal system at best. Persons accused of any offence punishable by sterilization could have the actions of their parents or grandparents held against them as evidence that they contain malignant DNA, and no quarter was given to those who had bastard children due to rape, they were classified as whores and prostitutes. texts.full_text: these devices can be complicated and difficult to manufacture or handle during assembly process which makes them expensive. Secondarily, some designs may not fully cover needle point after puncture; this could lead an accidental exposure risk for healthcare workers even with a 'safety' mechanism in place. Thirdy, there is often a tradeoff between the mechanical strength of safety features and their responsiveness - too strong can mean that it takes longer to deploy or requires more force which may be uncomfortable during use. Fourthly, these needles are generally only designed for single-use; they cannot safely retracted multiple times. This adds significant waste in terms both resources (materials used) as well health care costs. The present invention overcomes all of above-mentioned problems by providing a safer and more efficient safety indwelling needle with low manufacturing cost, simpler structure. In particular: 1). The design is simple yet effective; the mechanism can easily cover up pointed edge after puncture without requiring additional manipulation. 2) It has strong enough mechanical support to prevent accidental deployment during normal use while being responsive in case of false engagement attempts by users or other people (such as children). 3a). This needle assembly does not need any cap for covering; it is auto-capped automatically when withdrawal process begins. 4b.) The outer needle can be retracted multiple times without causing damage to device. 5c.). It also includes a lock mechanism that prevents accidental deployment even after the needle has been withdrawn from patient's body. This invention thus provides significant improvements in safety, simplicity of use and efficiency over conventional indwelling

texts.full_text: equities rally on Fed rate cut bets News Report Asian markets climbed Thursday and the dollar suffered further across-the-board losses after the head of the Federal Reserve all but guaranteed an interest rate cut this month. In the first day of closely watched congressional testimony, Jerome Powell said the case for lower borrowing costs “had strengthened” owing to headwinds caused by global trade uncertainty. The comments sparked much-needed relief on trading floors around the world, which had suffered steep losses at the start of the week in reaction to a blockbuster US jobs report that dented hopes for a steep cut at the Fed’s next policy meeting. US equities surged, with the Nasdaq ending at a record high and the greenback going into retreat, and the trend continued into Asia. Hong Kong jumped 0.8 percent and Shanghai added 0.1 percent. Tokyo stocks rose on Thursday, extending US rallies on growing expectations of a rate cut, while Nintendo surged after the game giant unveiled a scaled back, cheaper version of its Switch console. The benchmark Nikkei 225 indextook 0.4 percent to 21,387.69 in early trade, while the broader Topix index was up 0.3 percent at 1,594.96. The dollar was at 108.22 yen in early Asian trade, down from 108.39 yen in New York late Wednesday. Nintendo jumped 3.7 percent after the company unveiled a new Switch console that is smaller and cheaper than its existing model, as it seeks to boost sales of the popular gaming device. The new Switch Lite will be released on September 20, with a price tag of 19,980 yen ($183), compared with the existing model’s 29,980 yen. The announcement came as Nintendo reported a 14 percent fall in annual net profit to 116.5 billion yen ($1.07 billion) for the year to March, missing its own forecast of 120 billion yen. The company said it expected net profit to rise 13 percent to 130 billion yen in the current financial year.

She was the first woman in North Carolina to seek elected Office, when she ran for a municipal office in Asheville in 1899. Early life Helen Morris Lewis was born in Charleston, South Carolina, one of the ten children of John Williams Lewis and Anna Raven Vander Horst Lewis. She was a descendant of Lewis Morris, signer of the Declaration of Independence, and also of Arnoldus Vander Horst, an eighteenth-century South Carolina governor. The white oaks have a long history with humans and their use has been recorded since ancient Greece; it was used for shipbuilding in the early days of America. The wood is strong but not hard, so that when cut into boards or planks they are easily worked by hand tools such as planes (hence "white oak" which means white from being planed). It also makes good firewood and charcoal. The leaves on this tree can be used to make a tea called tisane. The bark of the trunk is sometimes ground up into powder form, then mixed with water or milk before drinking it as medicine for stomach problems like nausea , vomiting etc..
